beats.narration: An acoustic emission test is designed backward from two questions: what must you detect, and what will try to drown it out?

payload.title: WHAT SURVIVES THE THRESHOLD
payload.sub: AE listens for cracking metal — under a flood of plant noise.

beats.narration: Technique selection is one long trade. Attenuation rises with frequency, so a higher band escapes background noise but shortens every sensor's reach.

chips.s: High escapes noise, hears less far

beats.narration: The signal leaves the sensor as microvolts. A preamp adds forty decibels and band-filtering so it can survive a long cable to the system.
Then one decision makes the data: the threshold. A fixed line floods when noise climbs into it; a floating one rides a margin above the background.

payload.heading: Nothing below the threshold is data
payload.note: FIXED FLOODS WHEN NOISE CLIMBS · FLOATING HOLDS A MARGIN ABOVE BACKGROUND

beats.narration: What survives is judged on activity, intensity, and location. But remember: AE grades active sources. It never sizes them — that goes to UT.

payload.sub: AE finds and grades what's alive; another method measures it.
